ECCLESTON SUBMITS HIS


CASE TO CITY COUNCIL

pages.raw_text:

at the time and to others In au-

former this money was placed in
certificate of deposit In my name
as city treasurer, which certificate
and Its renewal, with other valu-
able papers, which my position
makes me custodian Of


Mr.. Cannon was shown both orig
inal and renewal of this certificate,
also original statement attached
thereto of facts and information
that this amount Of money was car
ried by the office in this manner,
and he was asked, If any items rep
resenting this amount were found,
to call my attention. was entirely
Ignored in the matter and biased
and prejudiced construction was
placed on my action, as per their
report.


On June 6, 1908, my report TO
cash receipts showed 81 long. This
amount of long cash" was, no
doubt. occasioned in the following
manner: Application for an ice
cream license, which is $25 per
quarter, was made at the office and
receipt No. 1904 was originally
made for this amount, the receipt
stub corresponding. Upon taking
this receipt to the comptroller the
applicant was informed that the
quarterly period for which licenses
are issued commences in Janray,
April, July and October, and that
he would be required to pay a pro-
portionate amount for the balance
| OF the month of June, which
amounted to 81. The receipt being
returned to the treasuer's office,
was changed. by the deputy max
ing it, from $25 to 1872, placing the
81 in the cash drawer, but neglect
ing to change the figures on the
stub to correspond with the receipt
Numerous causes may be assigned
for this error, but criminal intent


Is not one of them.


The items of $500 and $27.50 re.
spectively, and before referred to


with interest from March Is, 1907
evidenced by the certificate of de.
post in my possession have been
transferred to the general fund, to-
gether with the 81 occasioned by
error In receipt No. 13,104, as per
miscellaneous receipt No. 15,906-7
In their report calling attention


to water extension warrants, Nos


1887 to 1891, registered as of date


Feb. 10. 1908, in our warrant regis


ter, but not on back of warrants
